reviews.review_msg: Honestly started out liking her in class but as the semester went buy she starts to take a toll on you, she snaps at you and will often publicly shame you in class for checking your phone or eating something in class. Her classes are reading intensive from anywhere around 40-50 pages each week she likes to again shame you for not being able to do.

Honestly, I have never had a harder time in any of my classes and Nawal seems to make sure her students know her political ideas/agendas take precedence. In terms of grades, it would be easier to wrestle meat out an alligator's mouth than getting good grades from Nawal. Extremely displeased from my semester with her; she is an academic iceberg.

She is a caring professor but extremely authoritative. She loses her mind if you even glance at your phone in class and is very judgy with people who might be struggling in her class. Not supper accommodating, judgemental, authoritative, controlling, and rude.

Nawal was a great prof for 304! She explained the concepts clearly and would make sure everyone understood before she moved on. She was a little strict, but it didnt take away from the course itself. Would take another class with her

Nawal is a great lecturer and her class was one of my favourites! 
She is really talented at breaking down complex theories or ideas and making them more understandable.
She is also super nice and willing to help. When I went to her office hours she gave me tons of feedback on my paper proposal which really helped improve it.

Nawal is a caring prof, but as others have stated, very inflexible in terms of how students learn and is VERY opinionated (and will show). Problematic for dialogue as her politics can dominate and prevent deeper discussion. Sadly did not learn much from the course aside from the readings (from the early 2000s...) and quite dreaded the somber class.

Nawal truly is a great professor! I've seen some negative comments and I can say that's probably because you did something to disrupt her class. She's very passionate about the course material and wants you to do well. She's very clear about classroom policy and grading, she wants to see your understanding and research in papers and participation.

Nawal totally wasted students time! She controlled everything and did not allow different opinions! She gave poor feedback and did not care about students!!! She has parochial ideas! She is the most ridiculous and strict marker in the history of SFU! So so bad!!!!!! 0/10!!! definitely!

Nawal is a great professor. If you just come to class and takes notes of she says you can easily pass the course. She is not a tough grader, you gotta explain and be specific at the same time, so develop that and dont judge the teacher. She will try to help you as much as she can, she is very caring, she will explain you how to improve.

She's friendly but very authoritative. Tons of readings. Toughest grader I've ever seen although she will tell you how much easy the exams are and she wants you to do well. Wouldn't recommend to anyone. Sorry :(
